caseStudies.title: Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
caseStudies.description: Early warning system for supply chain disruptions.
caseStudies.overview: A multinational manufacturer needed visibility into supply chain risks across thousands of suppliers spanning 60 countries. Single-source dependencies, geopolitical risks, and natural disaster exposure were poorly understood, leaving the company vulnerable to costly disruptions.
caseStudies.problem: Supplier risk data was fragmented across procurement systems, spreadsheets, and informal knowledge. The 2021 shipping disruptions cost the company $200M in lost production due to component shortages that were not anticipated or visible until they had already materialised.
caseStudies.solution: A supply chain risk intelligence platform was built, integrating internal procurement data with external signals including news, logistics data, financial health indicators, and geopolitical risk scores. An interactive risk map allowed procurement teams to identify single-source dependencies and simulate disruption scenarios.

results.summary: The company successfully diversified its most critical supplier dependencies before several predicted disruptions materialised. The early warning capability has become a strategic advantage in procurement negotiations.

caseStudies.title: Operational Decision Platform
caseStudies.description: Real-time operational intelligence for a logistics network.
caseStudies.overview: A national logistics operator needed to move from reactive incident management to proactive operational optimization across a network of 400 distribution centers and 8,000 vehicles. Decisions were made locally with limited network-wide visibility.
caseStudies.problem: Route planning was performed statically each morning, with no mechanism to adapt to real-time conditions such as traffic, vehicle breakdowns, or surge demand. Dispatch coordinators operated in silos, unable to rebalance resources across regions during imbalances.
caseStudies.solution: A real-time operational platform was built, ingesting telemetry from all vehicles and facilities and presenting a unified operational picture. Dynamic route optimization re-planned deliveries in response to real-time conditions. Cross-region resource rebalancing recommendations were surfaced to senior coordinators with one-click deployment.

results.summary: Operational coordinators now manage the entire network from a single platform, with AI-assisted decision support that surfaces the right actions at the right time.

caseStudies.title: Fraud Analytics for Financial Crime
caseStudies.description: Graph-based transaction monitoring across a banking network.
caseStudies.overview: A regional bank's transaction monitoring system generated thousands of false positive alerts daily, overwhelming compliance investigators and causing genuine suspicious activity to be buried in noise. The goal was to dramatically improve detection quality while reducing investigator workload.
caseStudies.problem: The rule-based monitoring system flagged 4,200 alerts per day, of which fewer than 2% required escalation. Investigators spent most of their time triaging irrelevant alerts rather than investigating genuine risk. Complex multi-entity money laundering schemes involving shell companies were consistently missed.
caseStudies.solution: A graph-based analytics layer was built on top of the transaction data, modelling relationships between accounts, entities, and transactions. Machine learning models scored alerts by combining graph features with transaction patterns, dramatically reducing the alert volume. Network visualisation tools allowed investigators to explore entity relationships interactively.

results.summary: Investigators now focus exclusively on high-quality alerts, improving the bank's ability to detect genuine financial crime while dramatically reducing compliance costs.
